ServerDialogConnectionBasicsDesc: Choose the protocol, destination host, and service port Heimdall should open.

ServerDialogDirectConnectionDesc: No tunnel will be created for this server. Heimdall connects straight to the destination host and service port.

ServerDialogSshCredentialsDesc: These credentials are used for the SSH or SFTP session itself.
ServerDialogGatewayAuthDesc: The selected SSH gateway uses the credentials stored in its own gateway profile.

HelpShortcutsContent:
Navigation
  Ctrl+N	Add new server
  Ctrl+E	Edit selected server
  Ctrl+Del	Delete selected server
  Ctrl+F	Search servers
  Ctrl+B	Toggle sidebar
  F11	Toggle fullscreen

Connections
  Ctrl+K	Quick Connect
  Enter	Connect to selected server

Session
  Ctrl+Shift+S	Capture screenshot
  Ctrl+Shift+N	Network scanner

General
  F1	Show this help
  Esc	Exit fullscreen / close palette

ToolJsonErrorInputTooLarge: Input exceeds the 5 MB size limit.

ToolJwtErrorInvalidFormat: Invalid JWT format. A JWT must contain exactly 3 parts separated by dots.
ToolJwtErrorDecodeFailed: Failed to decode JWT. Check that the token is valid Base64Url.
ToolIpConvInputLabel: Enter IPv4 address (dotted, integer, hex, or binary)

ToolUrlEncComponentMode: Strict component encoding (encode all reserved characters)
ToolHttpFilterPlaceholder: Filter by code, name, or description...
ToolHttp100: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.
ToolHttp101: The server is switching protocols as requested by the client.
ToolHttp102: The server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.
ToolHttp103: Used to return some response headers before final HTTP message.
ToolHttp201: The request has been fulfilled and a new resource has been created.
ToolHttp202: The request has been accepted for processing, but the processing has not been completed.
ToolHttp203: The returned information is from a local or third-party copy, not the origin server.
ToolHttp204: The server successfully processed the request but is not returning any content.
ToolHttp205: The server successfully processed the request and asks the client to reset the document view.
ToolHttp206: The server is delivering only part of the resource due to a range header sent by the client.
ToolHttp207: A multi-status response conveys information about multiple resources.
ToolHttp208: Members of a DAV binding have already been enumerated and are not included again.
ToolHttp226: The server has fulfilled a GET request and the response represents one or more instance-manipulations.
ToolHttp300: The request has more than one possible response.
ToolHttp301: The requested resource has been permanently moved to a new URL.
ToolHttp302: The requested resource resides temporarily under a different URL.
ToolHttp303: The response can be found under a different URL using a GET method.
ToolHttp304: The resource has not been modified since the last request.
ToolHttp305: The requested resource must be accessed through the specified proxy.
ToolHttp307: The request should be repeated with another URL, but future requests should still use the original URL.
ToolHttp308: The request and all future requests should be repeated using another URL.
ToolHttp400: The server cannot process the request due to a client error.
ToolHttp401: Authentication is required and has failed or has not been provided.
ToolHttp402: Reserved for future use. Originally intended for digital payment schemes.
ToolHttp403: The client does not have permission to access the requested resource.
ToolHttp404: The server cannot find the requested resource.
ToolHttp405: The request method is not supported for the requested resource.
ToolHttp406: The requested resource is only capable of generating content not acceptable according to the Accept headers.
ToolHttp407: The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.
ToolHttp408: The server timed out waiting for the request.
ToolHttp409: The request could not be processed because of conflict in the current state of the resource.
ToolHttp410: The requested resource is no longer available and will not be available again.
ToolHttp411: The request did not specify the length of its content, which is required by the resource.
ToolHttp412: The server does not meet one of the preconditions specified in the request headers.
ToolHttp413: The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.
ToolHttp414: The URI provided was too long for the server to process.
ToolHttp415: The request entity has a media type which the server does not support.
ToolHttp416: The client has asked for a portion of the file that the server cannot supply.
ToolHttp417: The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.
ToolHttp418: This code was defined in 1998 as an April Fools' joke (RFC 2324).
ToolHttp421: The request was directed at a server that is not able to produce a response.
ToolHttp422: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.
ToolHttp423: The resource that is being accessed is locked.
ToolHttp424: The request failed because it depended on another request that failed.
ToolHttp425: The server is unwilling to risk processing a request that might be replayed.
ToolHttp426: The client should switch to a different protocol.
ToolHttp428: The origin server requires the request to be conditional.
ToolHttp429: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.
ToolHttp431: The server is unwilling to process the request because its header fields are too large.
ToolHttp451: The resource is unavailable for legal reasons.
ToolHttp500: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.
ToolHttp501: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.
ToolHttp502: The server received an invalid response from the upstream server.
ToolHttp503: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to temporary overloading or maintenance.
ToolHttp504: The server did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.
ToolHttp505: The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.
ToolHttp506: Transparent content negotiation results in a circular reference.
ToolHttp507: The server is unable to store the representation needed to complete the request.
ToolHttp508: The server detected an infinite loop while processing the request.
ToolHttp510: Further extensions to the request are required for the server to fulfill it.
ToolHttp511: The client needs to authenticate to gain network access.
ToolDiffStatusTooLarge: Input exceeds {0} lines. Please reduce the text size.
ToolCertErrorInvalidPort: Invalid port number. Must be between 1 and 65535.
ToolCronDescEveryHour: Runs at the start of every hour
ToolCronDescMonthlyAt: Runs on the {0}th of every month at {1}
ToolCronValidationFieldCount: A cron expression must have exactly 5 fields separated by spaces
ToolCronValidationOutOfRange: Field "{0}" values must be between {1} and {2}
ToolSshKeyGenPassphraseHint: Optional. Encrypts the private key with AES-256-CBC.
ToolSshKeyGenEd25519Notice: Ed25519 support coming soon (pending .NET runtime support).
ToolValidationHostRequired: Please enter a hostname or IP address.
ToolValidationPortRangeRequired: Please enter a port or port range.
ToolPingValidationTimeout: Invalid timeout value. Please enter a number between 100 and 30000 ms.
ToolPingValidationCount: Invalid count value. Please enter a number between 0 and 100000 (0 = unlimited).
